summary: Full E2E test of new Daily Status Report feature — on-demand PDF endpoint, recipients endpoint, scheduler registry (3 jobs), on-demand cron trigger, derive_stage_and_next unit cases, collector helper, and frontend sidebar download. 10/10 pytest cases + frontend Playwright run all green.
critical_code_review_comments: scheduler._daily_status_email imports from server (`collect_daily_status_data`) at runtime — acceptable to avoid circular import, but be aware that any import failure silently turns into a 200/`error: collector_unavailable` response; consider surfacing as a 5xx in /run admin route so failures aren't masked.
collect_daily_status_data has no upper bound for `deals.find` other than `to_list(5000)` — fine today (9 active deals) but flag for future scale.
Mongo selector `{"$ne": "", "$lt": today}` in overdue_tasks query relies on `$lt` being evaluated against the same string field — works for ISO dates but worth a comment.
daily_status_pdf.derive_stage_and_next uses f-string `%-d` which is Linux-only. App runs on Linux containers so safe, but worth a comment for portability.

/api/reports/daily-status.pdf is open to any logged-in user (no admin gate). Spec says auth-only which matches; just noting that non-admin field reps can see the full company pipeline — confirm that's desired.
seed_data_creation: None; reused existing 9 active deals + tomorrow Roof Walk event.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_daily_status.py uses pytest-asyncio (installed via pip in this iteration) with --asyncio-mode=auto. It verifies the public HTTPS API plus exercises the Python helpers directly via sys.path.insert(/app/backend). PDF section assertions use pypdf 6.x. Frontend test uses page.expect_download() to assert filename 'Daily Status - YYYY-MM-DD.pdf' and PDF magic bytes. Recipients endpoint correctly returns just [darren@sealtechsolutions.co] now that admin@roofingcrm.com is soft-deleted.
